page: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-96xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server instance featuring dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. The system is equipped with 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It provides 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance features integrated local storage consisting of 22800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It also delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and high-speed networking supports data-intensive operations without external storage bottlenecks.

Given its bare-metal architecture and massive resource density, the r8id.metal-96xl offers qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads that require direct hardware access and high memory-to-core ratios. This server is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and heavy data-processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-96xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by Intel x86_64 processors, it features 192 physical cores and 384 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB. For storage-heavy operations, the instance includes 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for workloads requiring direct hardware access. The r8id.metal-96xl is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data processing, and enterprise-grade applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-96xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB RAM, 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for enterprise databases.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and dedicated high-frequency processors for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 384 dedicated vCPUs (192 physical cores) powered by Intel x86_64 architecture at 3.9 GHz.
3072.0 GB of system memory optimized for large-scale in-memory applications.
22800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfers.
Bare-metal deployment model eliminating virtualization overhead.
Designed for enterprise databases, in-memory analytics, and heavy data-processing pipelines.
